Fear and Greed is at
15
(Extreme
Fear)
and BTC is at
$59K
this morning after dropping to $58K yesterday. The specific
trigger was the PCE inflation reading for May, which came in at
4.1% year-over-year, the highest in three years, sending the
Nasdaq down 2% within 30 minutes and triggering over $600 million
in crypto liquidations in a single hour.

This is no longer a slow grind lower. This is a named event with
a named consequence. The market cap has dropped below $2.1T.
Strategy preferred shares hit an all-time low. And analysts are
now debating publicly whether $55K is next or whether this is the
capitulation floor.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Multicoin Just Set a Bold Target for HYPE by 2028

Multicoin Capital projected HYPE could reach approximately
$319 by 2028, more than 400% from current levels. The firm said
the market is pricing HYPE too narrowly as a crypto derivatives
exchange rather than a broader multi-asset platform.

Multicoin said HYPE is now one of its largest holdings.

What annual earnings figure does Multicoin use to arrive at its
price target?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/pqsNv-bDrpCFxeXH_xHQKg~~/AAQRxRA~/B0IacHrhgCjsR5eo3E_1fp3KKNgJugcGpPcM_WSf2vpaZFR-rvzr891lFgJBGwaNPY0wmd8nkwcvmzYuZNHyio4nLZerLBhauLmMpRExCX-iQWk2FkLJ_jADG5yg8s7yKScsCJN4YOOb0wUK2WH6v56Jf1TRyTXBMSaEIHonP8s6XKZObPiIrLwKj-HHYf0ITp5-xBZOPtHS6_qazU3vGoYla9UKv_O

75102.subject: Crypto Biz: The cost of stacking sats
75102.body:
Crypto Biz Newsletter

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Cointelegraph


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Cointelegraph

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/crypto-biz-cost-stacking-sats-cointelegraph-hjaxc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BRb1FHkNcSUOLXUaFK2%2B6tA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1gBKPs23IFjIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqus26vj~9g-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqus26vj-9g
  
This week, crypto analytics company CryptoQuant challenged the prevailing narrative around Michael SaylorвҖҷs Strategy, urging the company to pause Bitcoin purchases and rebuild its cash reserves. The warning came after its dividend coverage fell to just 14 months from roughly seven years. Strategy isnвҖҷt facing an immediate cash crunch, but CryptoQuantвҖҷs warning puts the spotlight on the financing structure behind its Bitcoin strategy. With cash reserves shrinking and dividend obligations increasing, StrategyвҖҷs ability to keep funding new purchases is drawing closer scrutiny. The rest of this weekвҖҷs Crypto Biz shows how the industry is evolving. CBOE is eyeing perpetual Bitcoin and Ether futures, Chainlink is working with European and Korean banks on stablecoin-based FX settlement and Zcash miner Fortitude is heading to Nasdaq through an unlikely merger with a healthcare company. CryptoQuant urges Strategy to pause Bitcoin buying as dividend coverage drops to 14 months Earlier this week, CryptoQuant argued that StrategyвҖҷs aggressive Bitcoin accumulation has become increasingly difficult to sustain, urging the company to rebuild its cash reserves after dividend coverage fell to just 14 months from roughly seven years. CEO Ki Young Ju said the StrategyвҖҷs cash position has deteriorated as annual dividend obligations surged to $1.2 billion following large issuances of STRC preferred shares carrying an 11.5% yield. While StrategyвҖҷs cash reserve recovered to about $1.4 billion after recent MSTR share sales, it remains down 38% year-to-date after the company repurchased $1.5 billion of its 2029 senior notes. The warning comes as StrategyвҖҷs funding model faces additional pressure. STRC preferred shares recently fell as much as 17.5% below their $100 par value, limiting the companyвҖҷs ability to raise fresh capital through additional preferred stock sales.В  StrategyвҖҷs cash reserve and dividend coverage. Source: CryptoQuant Continue ReadingвҖҰ CBOE considers converting Bitcoin and Ether futures into perpetual contracts The Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE) is weighing a plan to convert its continuous Bitcoin (BTC and Ether (ETH) futures into perpetual futures, according to a Wall Street Journal report. The potential move follows recent regulatory changes after the US Commodity Futures Trading Commission approved crypto perpetual futures for Kalshi and
